summary: PR #2615 attempted to link src/melee/gm/gmopening.c by adjusting gmopening source data declarations plus GALE01 symbols and splits metadata. The author reported that all functions matched in objdiff, but marking the file as Matching caused a data mismatch.

key_files.role: Main gmopening source changes: reorganized static data declarations, replaced pointer-derived format string accesses with inline string literals, added explicit address comments, added sdata2_order and force_active unused_floats data-order helpers.
Symbol metadata change splitting gm_803DBF78 from one 0x3C global object into a 0x24 local float object plus two local string labels at 0x803DBF9C and 0x803DBFA8.
Split metadata change moving .bss range 0x80480B38-0x80480D58 from melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c to melee/gm/gmopening.c.

what_changed.text: gmopening gained explicit static declarations near the top for rodata Vec3 values, PerfLabelLine gm_80480B38, sbss globals gm_804D67D0 through gm_804D67F4, and gm_803DBFB4 with address comments.
The previous static struct gm_803DBF78 combining nine floats and two 12-byte strings was removed from source.
fn_801A9FCC no longer forms a u8* pointer to gm_803DBF78 and adds offsets 0x24/0x30 for sprintf formats; it now uses inline format literals "\cffff00%2d" and "\cffff00%3d".
The PR added a no-call sdata2_order function containing casts of 82.0f and 290.0f to void, apparently to affect sdata2 literal ordering, but no PR comment explains the exact need.
The PR added a #pragma push / #pragma force_active on / #pragma pop block around static float unused_floats[] containing the nine former gm_803DBF78 float values.
symbols.txt changed gm_803DBF78 metadata from a 0x3C global object into a 0x24 local float object and introduced lbl_803DBF9C and lbl_803DBFA8 as 0xC string labels.
splits.txt narrowed gm_1A4C.c .bss to end at 0x80480B38 and added gmopening.c .bss ownership from 0x80480B38 to 0x80480D58.

smart_moves.text: Replacing format-string pointer arithmetic through a mixed data blob with inline format string literals improved readability and aligns with the loaded standards' preference for avoiding raw pointer math and not promoting ordinary string literals into symbols without ownership evidence.
The PR adjusted symbols.txt and splits.txt together with source changes, reflecting that section ownership and symbol metadata can be part of matching when source data layout changes.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a decompiled data object mixes floats and strings only to support offset-based string accesses, consider whether symbol metadata should describe separate float and string objects while source code keeps ordinary format strings inline.

A file can have text-section objdiff success while still being blocked from Matching by data-section layout or split metadata issues; record the data mismatch separately instead of claiming complete matching.
When moving .bss ownership between adjacent game-mode translation units, update splits.txt ranges at the boundary and keep corresponding source declarations in the owning TU.

naming_conventions.text: The PR kept address-style names such as gm_803DBF78, lbl_803DBF9C, gm_804D67D0, and fn_801A9FCC rather than inventing semantic names.
New label-style names lbl_803DBF9C and lbl_803DBFA8 were used for newly split string objects, preserving address identity without asserting semantic meaning.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used #pragma force_active on around unused_floats[] to keep otherwise unused float data emitted.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded standards treat pragmas and force-active style data retention as exceptional and evidence-sensitive; this PR gives no detailed verifier log beyond the author's objdiff/data-mismatch note.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Added sdata2_order with unused float literals 82.0f and 290.0f, likely as a data/literal ordering tactic.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The exact matching effect is not explained in comments or review evidence, so this should be treated as a narrow historical tactic rather than a general standard.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split one data symbol into separate float and string symbol metadata entries to better represent .data layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Supported by the diff, but no external map/symbol evidence is included in the PR slice.

follow_up_queries: gmopening data mismatch Matching gm_803DBF78 unused_floats

notes: Strong evidence for changed files and exact diff contents.
The PR body explicitly claims objdiff text matching and a data mismatch when marked Matching, but no objdiff, checkdiff, or build logs are included in the slice.
There are no issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the supplied metadata, so reviewer intent and acceptance rationale are unavailable.
The purpose of sdata2_order and force_active unused_floats is inferred from the code shape and PR title/body, not directly explained by the author.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Text objdiff success can still be blocked by data mismatch
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2615 records a gmopening case where the author said everything matched in objdiff but marking the file as Matching caused a data mismatch. Treat text-section closure and data-section readiness as separate claims.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid pointer-derived format strings when literals are ordinary source strings
accepted_candidate_records.text: In gmopening, sprintf format arguments were changed from offsets into gm_803DBF78 to inline literals "\cffff00%2d" and "\cffff00%3d" while symbols.txt split the surrounding data object. This is a concrete example of replacing generated-looking pointer math to strings with direct string literals when source evidence supports it.

source_update_candidates.title: gmopening owns bss range 0x80480B38-0x80480D58 in PR #2615 split metadata
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2615 changed config/GALE01/splits.txt so melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c .bss ends at 0x80480B38 and melee/gm/gmopening.c gains .bss start:0x80480B38 end:0x80480D58.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path/split ownership fact; curator should verify against current splits before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_803DBF78 split into float data plus two string labels in PR #2615
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2615 changed symbols.txt metadata at 0x803DBF78 from one 0x3C global object into gm_803DBF78 as a 0x24 local float object, lbl_803DBF9C as a 0xC string, and lbl_803DBFA8 as a 0xC string.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential symbol metadata fact; curator should verify current symbol state and section ownership before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR #2615 as an example for separating text matches from data readiness
source_update_candidates.text: This PR is a concrete example for existing text-before-data and verification-ledger standards: an author-reported objdiff text match did not mean the file could safely be marked Matching because data mismatch remained.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could be added as an illustrative example under existing standards, not as a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote force_active unused_floats or sdata2_order dummy functions as accepted general tactics from this PR alone; the slice lacks detailed verifier evidence and loaded standards require narrow justification for pragmas and data-order tactics.
Do not claim gmopening was ultimately marked Matching from this slice; the PR body says doing so caused a data mismatch.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; there were no review comments or reviews in the supplied PR evidence.
